summary: Adding case_sensitive parameter to groupby() filter (sync+async)

type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
summary: Adding with_counts param to groupby filter (do_groupby)

type: QUESTION
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
summary: Both editing do_groupby - I'm adding with_counts (grouper,items,count) tuples; propose I add my param after yours lands, or we each add independent optional kwargs with clear order. Can you share your diff/approach so I can merge cleanly?

type: STATUS
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
summary: Added with_counts=False kwarg (appended after default param) to sync_do_groupby/do_groupby, new _GroupTupleWithCount class; tests pass. Please add case_sensitive as an appended kwarg too so our diffs merge cleanly without reordering params.

type: ANSWER
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
summary: I've completed case_sensitive param on sync_do_groupby/do_groupby: signature is (environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False). Add your with_counts as a new kwarg after case_sensitive (e.g. case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False) in both sync_do_groupby and do_groupby; keep my case-insensitive grouping logic (uses ignore_case postprocess + output_expr remap for original-case key) intact and layer your counts on top of the final out list. I'm about to write patch.txt now.

type: STATUS
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
summary: My working copy only shows my with_counts edit (no case_sensitive changes visible), so we appear to be on separate copies - each submitting our own patch.txt independently is fine. My with_counts implementation is complete, tests pass (837/837), patch.txt written.

type: STATUS
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
summary: case_sensitive already appended as last kwarg after default (default=None, case_sensitive=False) in both sync_do_groupby/do_groupby, no reordering needed on my end. My patch.txt is finalized and tests pass (837 passed).
